name: bnd
title: bnd
summary: tool to create and diagnose OSGi bundles
description:
tool to create and diagnose OSGi bundles
The bnd tool helps you create and diagnose OSGi bundles.
The key functions are:
.
* Show the manifest and JAR contents of a bundle
* Wrap a JAR so that it becomes a bundle
* Create a Bundle from a specification and a class path
* Verify the validity of the manifest entries
.
The tool is capable of acting as:
.
* Command line tool
* Maven Plugin
* Ant Plugin

arguments.raw: --output  Specify the output file path. The default is output.jar in the current directory

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bnd because tool to create and diagnose OSGi bundles.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bnd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bnd [general-options] [cmd] [cmd-options]` as documented upstream for bnd.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bnd [general-options] <file>.jar` as documented upstream for bnd.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bnd [general-options] <file>.bnd` as documented upstream for bnd.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bnd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bnd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bnd. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bnd [general-options] <file>.jar`. Further invocations from upstream: `bnd [general-options] <file>.bnd`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: tool to create and diagnose OSGi bundles.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: tool to create and diagnose OSGi bundles.
